Ñetas.description: The Ñetas are a prison gang founded in 1979 at Oso Blanco prison in Río Piedras, Puerto Rico, by Carlos Torres Iriarte. Originally formed for mutual protection and to improve prison conditions, they later engaged in drug trafficking, robbery, and extortion. The gang operates in Puerto Rico, the United States, Spain, and Ecuador, with an estimated 12,000 members. Despite the death of their founder, they remain active and are known for their white color identification.

descriptions: Big Circle Gang is allied with Netas.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Netas

descriptions: Ñetas is listed as a rival of Latin Kings.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Crips , Dominicans Don't Play , Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , Latin Eagles , Maniac Latin Disciples , MS-13 , Ñetas , La Raza Nation , Simon City Royals , Los Solidos , Spanish Cobras , Tiny Rascal Gang , United Blood Nation

descriptions: Ñetas are main rivals of Latin Kings in Spain.
edges.evidence_quote: In Spain, the Ñetas are the main rivals of the Latin Kings.

En el país andino , tras pocos años de violenta actividad, ambas bandas llegaron a un acuerdo de paz (solo en Ecuador) y cese de actividades en 2009.

Kerryonians.description: The Kerryonians were an Irish-American street gang based in New York City's Five Points area, founded in the 1820s by immigrants from County Kerry, Ireland. They were the second oldest criminal street gang in New York City and were known for targeting British-descended New Yorkers and disrupting a performance by actor William Charles Macready in 1849. The gang was eventually absorbed into larger Five Points gangs such as the Dead Rabbits, Roach Guards, and Chichesters.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chichesters, Tammany Hall, Plug Uglies, Roach Guards, Mulberry Street Boys, Municipal Police, Forty Thieves, Shirt Tails, Kerryonians

7th Street Gang.description: A street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded in 2000 by Efrain 'Cheko' Hidalgo. The gang was involved in drug trafficking of cocaine, heroin, and other narcotics, and engaged in violent rivalry with the 10th Street Gang. Many members were arrested and convicted under RICO charges, leading to the gang's dissolution by 2017.

descriptions: The 10th Street Gang had a rivalry with the 7th Street Gang, leading to violence and homicides.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang started garnering infamy when they started having rivalries with a newly established gang, the 7th Street gang.

descriptions: The 7th Street Gang and 10th Street Gang were rivals, engaging in violent conflicts.
edges.evidence_quote: They were heated rivals of the 10th street gang.
